## What Happened

Prevented false removal or merging of SO (Solution Orientation) and SR (Stakeholder Representation) signals. These are actually moderately independent at story-level (r=0.297 and 0.389 with HRCB), contradicting Phase A domain-level ranking correlation.

## The Mechanism

Re-checked Phase A findings about SO/SR redundancy (ρ=0.609, 0.582) by re-analyzing with story-level Pearson correlation instead of domain-level Spearman. Discovered high correlation was an artifact of aggregation method and statistical technique, not substantive redundancy.

## Why It Matters

Domain-level aggregation inflates correlations because averaging reduces noise. When validation shows high signal correlation at one aggregation level, triangulate with lower-level data and alternative statistical methods before declaring redundancy. This principle prevents false construct mergers and unnecessary signal elimination in multi-level validation.

## Counterfactual

Without this trigger: Without re-checking with alternative method, would have implemented Phase A decision to flag SO/SR as redundant and merged or removed one signal, losing legitimate independent measurement dimensions from the construct.
